title: The Shepherd at the Tower of the Flock
narration: It is the fourth watch. The deepest fold of the night.
We keep the flock here, by the old tower between Bethlehem and Jerusalem — the place men call the tower of the flock, where Rachel lies buried.
We are awake. We are always awake at this hour, watching, guarding the sheep against the wolf in the dark.
Then one is standing beside us. Right there. A man in a white garment, his face shining, glad.
And the brightness of God breaks over us — over my body, my face, and somehow inside me too, in the mind, as if a sun had risen in the dark.
I am afraid. We are all afraid, a great fear, at this sudden light, this thing we have never seen.
He says: Do not be afraid.
He says: I bring you good news. A great joy. For all the people.
Today, he says — to you — a Savior is born, in the city of David. He is Christ the Lord.
To us. The poor ones. The simple ones. The ones who keep watch in the cold.
And he gives us a sign: you will find a child, wrapped in swaddling cloths, laid in a manger.
Not in gold. Not in silk. Swaddling bands, and an animals' trough.
Then — suddenly — he is not alone.
The whole night fills with them. A multitude of the heavenly host, the army of heaven, ranged around him.
And with one voice they sing: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace to men of good will.
The night is lit like day. The dark is gone, burned away by the brightness of God.
Then they rise from us into heaven, the singing climbing with them, until the sky is empty and still.
And I say to the others — we all say it — let us go over to Bethlehem. Let us see this thing the Lord has made known to us.
We do not walk. We run. Driven by the joy of it, the longing to see the child.
And we find them — Mary, and Joseph, and the infant laid in the manger, just as he told us.
The doors are not shut. For peace lies over all the earth tonight, and the town is full of strangers; so we come in by night and stand before him.
I look on him with my own eyes. The child in the straw. And I know — God is here, laid low among the beasts, for us.
We go back to the flock with an enormous joy, glorifying and praising God for all we have heard and seen, just as it was told to us.
prayer: Glory to you, O God, in the highest,
and on earth, peace to men of good will.
You sent your light into our cold watch,
and did not pass over the poor, the simple, the watching ones.
Let me run, as those shepherds ran, with no sloth and no delay,
toward the child laid low in the manger for me.
And let me return, glorifying and praising you with heart and mouth,
for all that I have heard, and all that you have shown.
Glory to you in the highest, O God; on earth, your peace. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Vigilantibus pastoribus annuntiatur gaudium lucis, et illis dicitur esse natum Salvatorem, pauperibus atque laborantibus.
latinJewel.english: To the watching shepherds the joy of light is announced, and to them — the poor and the laboring — it is said that a Savior is born.
